name: Reliance Foundation Undergraduate Scholarships
description: Supports meritorious students from diverse backgrounds across India to pursue undergraduate education in any stream of their choice.
whoCanApply: First-year students enrolled in full-time regular undergraduate degree courses.
eligibility: Class 12 passed with minimum 60% marks. Must be a citizen of India.
incomeCriteria: Family income less than ₹15 Lakhs per annum (preference to < ₹2.5 Lakhs).
benefits: Up to ₹2 Lakhs award over the duration of the degree course + grooming and mentoring sessions.

name: HDFC Bank Parivartan's ECS Scholarship
description: Financial support to meritorious and needy students from underprivileged sections of society, ensuring they can complete school or degree education.
whoCanApply: Students pursuing Class 6 to 12, Diploma, UG, PG, and professional courses (B.Tech, MBBS, MBA).
eligibility: Scored at least 55% in the previous qualifying examination. Facing economic crisis.
incomeCriteria: Family income less than ₹2.5 Lakhs per annum.
benefits: Up to ₹75,000 per annum for professional courses; up to ₹30,000 for regular courses.

name: Tata Capital Pankh Scholarship
description: An initiative to support students belonging to economically weaker sections of society to continue their education.
whoCanApply: Students enrolled in Class 11, Class 12, or professional graduation courses (B.Tech, B.Com, B.Sc).
eligibility: Scored at least 60% in previous exam. Indian nationality only.
incomeCriteria: Family income less than ₹4 Lakhs per annum.
benefits: Covers up to 80% of tuition fees (ranges from ₹10,000 to ₹12,000 for school, up to ₹50,000 for college).

name: LIC Golden Jubilee Scholarship Scheme
description: Uplifts students from economically backward families, providing opportunities for higher education in medicine, engineering, or regular graduation.
whoCanApply: Students passed Class 12 (or equivalent) and studying in government-recognized degree, diploma, or vocational fields.
eligibility: Scored minimum 60% in Class 12 or equivalent. Pursuing regular courses only.
incomeCriteria: Family income less than ₹2.5 Lakhs per annum.
benefits: ₹20,000 per annum for degree students; ₹10,000 per annum for vocational/diploma programs.

name: Kotak Kanya Scholarship Scheme
description: Financial assistance to meritorious girl students from low-income families, enabling them to complete higher professional education.
whoCanApply: Meritorious girl students admitted to first-year professional graduation courses (Engineering, Medical, Law, Architecture).
eligibility: Scored 85% or above in Class 12 board examinations. Resident of India.
incomeCriteria: Family income less than ₹6 Lakhs per annum.
benefits: ₹1.5 Lakhs per year towards academic expenses (tuition fee, hostel charges, food, books, laptops).

name: Bharti Airtel Scholarship Scheme
description: Empowers underprivileged youths by providing access to technical graduation courses in top institutes like IITs.
whoCanApply: First-year students enrolled in Engineering and STEM degrees.
incomeCriteria: Family income less than ₹3 Lakhs per annum.
benefits: Covers tuition fees, hostel charges, and pocket allowance throughout the graduation program.

name: Aditya Birla Scholarship Program
description: Prestigious award aiming to foster leadership qualities among outstanding students in premier engineering, law, and business institutes.
whoCanApply: Students enrolled in elite institutes (IITs, BITS Pilani, IIMs, National Law Universities).
eligibility: Must apply through their respective premium institutions. Selected based on general academic merit and interview grids.
incomeCriteria: Based strictly on Institute Merit; no separate lower income cap, though EWS status is considered.
benefits: ₹65,000 to ₹1,75,000 per annum towards tuition and board charges.

name: Santoor Scholarship Program
description: Financial support for girl students from underprivileged backgrounds in Andhra Pradesh, Telangana, and Karnataka to pursue higher education.
whoCanApply: Girl students who have passed Class 12 and want to pursue a higher education course of minimum 3 years duration.
eligibility: Must belong to AP, Telangana, or Karnataka. Pass Class 10 & 12 in a government school.
incomeCriteria: Family income less than ₹4 Lakhs per annum.
benefits: ₹24,000 per annum to cover tuition fees, books, and living expenses.

name: Foundation For Excellence (FFE) Scholarship
description: Provides financial aid to exceptionally smart students facing financial constraints to study Engineering, Technology, or Medicine.
whoCanApply: First-year students pursuing B.Tech, Integrated M.Tech, Dual Degree, or MBBS.
eligibility: JEE/NEET rank must be within top limits. Class 12 pass with >= 70% marks.
incomeCriteria: Family income less than ₹3 Lakhs per annum.
benefits: ₹40,000 per annum + skill training + corporate placement mentorship.

name: L&T Build India Scholarship (BIS)
description: Spurs core engineering career tracks. Selects candidates for M.Tech in Construction Technology & Management at IIT Madras/Delhi/Guwahati.
whoCanApply: Final-year civil or electrical engineering undergraduate students.
eligibility: B.E./B.Tech civil/electrical with >= 65% aggregate. Sponsorship format based on exam assessment.
incomeCriteria: Based strictly on engineering merit and hiring standard rules.
benefits: Fully-funded M.Tech program at premier IIT + monthly stipend + guaranteed job placement in L&T upon course completion.
documents: Letter of endorsement from College placement

name: Infosys Foundation Samarpan Scholarship
description: Supports students from economically backward categories to pursue degree courses in computers, IT, and software fields.
whoCanApply: Students enrolled in B.Tech/B.Sc computer streams with verified merit.
eligibility: Minimum 70% in Class 12. Must study in recognized government or private colleges.
incomeCriteria: Family income less than ₹3.5 Lakhs per annum.
benefits: ₹40,000 per annum towards fees, laptops purchase, or hostel charges.

name: Wipro Foundation Santoor Scholarship Scheme
description: Provides financial aid to girls from state government schools to complete their higher education.
whoCanApply: Girls pursuing any degree or diploma course after finishing Class 12.
eligibility: Belong to AP/TS/KA. Completed Class 12 from government schools.
incomeCriteria: Family income less than ₹3.5 Lakhs per annum.
benefits: ₹24,000 per annum to clear college expenses and board fees.

name: Amazon Future Engineer India Scholarship
description: Uplifts underprivileged girl students by providing financial assistance, mentorship, and opportunities to build careers in computer science.
whoCanApply: Girl students enrolled in first-year of B.E./B.Tech courses in computer science, IT, or related technical branches.
eligibility: Admitted via state/central counseling. Scored good grades in Class 12 exams.
incomeCriteria: Family income less than ₹3 Lakhs per annum.
benefits: ₹50,000 per year towards tuition fees + laptop + direct engineering mentorship from Amazon developers + interview training.

name: Google Scholarships (India)
description: Supports computer science and technology students with financial assistance and developer community resources.
whoCanApply: Students enrolled in computer science or related tech courses.
eligibility: Enrolled in a regular computer science bachelor's degree program.
benefits: $2,500 USD award to support education expenses.
